---
title: "API Wrappers"
---

API wrappers talk to the `daggle serve --port` REST API. They require the httr2 package.

## Base URL resolution

The base URL is resolved in this order:

1. Explicit `base_url` parameter passed to the function
2. `DAGGLE_API_URL` environment variable
3. `http://127.0.0.1:8787` (default)

## Function reference

| Function | Endpoint | Returns |
|----------|----------|---------|
| `health()` | GET /api/v1/health | list |
| `list_dags()` | GET /api/v1/dags | data.frame |
| `get_dag(name)` | GET /api/v1/dags/{name} | list |
| `trigger(name, params)` | POST /api/v1/dags/{name}/run | list |
| `list_runs(name)` | GET /api/v1/dags/{name}/runs | data.frame |
| `get_run(name, run_id)` | GET /api/v1/dags/{name}/runs/{run_id} | list |
| `get_outputs(name, run_id)` | GET /api/v1/dags/{name}/runs/{run_id}/outputs | data.frame |
| `get_step_log(name, run_id, step_id)` | GET /api/v1/dags/{name}/runs/{run_id}/steps/{step_id}/log | list |
| `cancel_run(name, run_id)` | POST /api/v1/dags/{name}/runs/{run_id}/cancel | list |
| `approve(name, run_id, step_id)` | POST /api/v1/dags/{name}/runs/{run_id}/steps/{step_id}/approve | list |
| `reject(name, run_id, step_id)` | POST /api/v1/dags/{name}/runs/{run_id}/steps/{step_id}/reject | list |
| `cleanup(older_than)` | POST /api/v1/runs/cleanup | list |

All functions accept an optional `base_url` parameter.

List endpoints (`list_dags()`, `list_runs()`, `get_outputs()`) parse JSON with `simplifyVector = TRUE`, so the flat arrays returned by the API become data.frames directly.

## Usage examples

### List DAGs

```r
library(daggleR)

dags <- list_dags()
dags
#>       name steps   schedule last_status            last_run
#> 1 etl-daily     4 0 3 * * *     success 2025-01-15T03:00:12Z
#> 2   reports     2       <NA>     running 2025-01-15T10:30:00Z
```

### Trigger and poll

```r
result <- trigger("etl-daily", params = list(date = "2025-01-15"))
run_id <- result$run_id

# Poll until complete
repeat {
  run <- get_run("etl-daily", run_id)
  if (run$status %in% c("success", "failure", "cancelled")) break
  Sys.sleep(2)
}

run$status
#> [1] "success"
```

### Get outputs

```r
outputs <- get_outputs("etl-daily", "latest")
outputs
#>     step_id          key                          value
#> 1     fetch    row_count                           1432
#> 2 transform  output_path /tmp/transformed.parquet
#> 3  validate       passed                           true
```

### View step logs

```r
log <- get_step_log("etl-daily", "latest", "fetch")
cat(log$stdout)
#> Fetching data from API...
#> Received 1432 rows.
```

### Approve a waiting step

```r
approve("etl-daily", run_id = "latest", step_id = "load")
```

### Health check

```r
health()
#> $status
#> [1] "ok"
#>
#> $version
#> [1] "0.5.0"
#>
#> $uptime_seconds
#> [1] 3621
```

### Clean up old runs

```r
cleanup("30d")
#> $removed
#> [1] 42
#>
#> $freed_bytes
#> [1] 10485760
#>
#> $freed
#> [1] "10.0 MB"
```
